label: grief_response
evidence_text:
Some people throw everything away when they lose the thing they love most—burn it all, scatter the ashes, start fresh with empty hands.
---
Others keep every thread, every cracked vessel, every scrap of parchment with nothing written on it, and pull it tighter and tighter until there is no room left to breathe.
---
Aviel was one of those. Which is relevant because it is exactly what his son was creeping through the dark to undo.

evidence.quote: Some people throw everything away when they lose the thing they love most—burn it all, scatter the ashes, start fresh with empty hands.
Others keep every thread, every cracked vessel, every scrap of parchment with nothing written on it, and pull it tighter and tighter until there is no room left to breathe.
Aviel was one of those. Which is relevant because it is exactly what his son was creeping through the dark to undo.

interpretation: The dualism contrasts two ways people cope with grief: some discard all remnants of what they loved, starting fresh with emptiness, while others cling tightly to every fragment, leaving no space to breathe.

observation.summary: The dualism contrasts two ways people cope with grief: some discard all remnants of what they loved, starting fresh with emptiness, while others cling tightly to every fragment, leaving no space to breathe.
